query: Spain national football team 2026 World Cup form squad strength FIFA ranking
answer:
The research agent has returned comprehensive findings. Let me compile the final structured output.

## Spain National Football Team — 2026 FIFA World Cup Research Brief

### 1. FIFA World Ranking
- **Current Ranking: #2** (as of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking release of 11 June 2026)
- Spain held the **#1 ranking from September 2025 through April 2026**, before slipping to #2 behind Argentina.
- Spain was seeded in **Pot 1** for the 2026 World Cup draw (November 2025, when ranked #1).
- Notably, Spain became only the **second nation ever** (after Germany) to simultaneously hold #1 in both men's and women's FIFA rankings (Sept 2025–Apr 2026).

### 2. Recent Match Results

#### 2025 (10 matches: W6 D3 L1*)
| Date | Competition | Match | Score |
|------|------------|-------|-------|
| 20 Mar | Nations League QF 1st leg | Netherlands vs Spain | 2-2 |
| 23 Mar | Nations League QF 2nd leg | Spain vs Netherlands | 3-3 (Spain win 5-4 pens) |
| 5 Jun | Nations League SF | Spain vs France | 5-4 |
| 8 Jun | Nations League Final | Portugal vs Spain | 2-2 (Portugal win 5-3 pens) |
| 4 Sep | WCQ | Bulgaria vs Spain | 0-3 |
| 7 Sep | WCQ | Turkey vs Spain | 0-6 |
| 11 Oct | WCQ | Spain vs Georgia | 2-0 |
| 14 Oct | WCQ | Spain vs Bulgaria | 4-0 |
| 15 Nov | WCQ | Georgia vs Spain | 0-4 |
| 18 Nov | WCQ | Spain vs Turkey | 2-2 |

*\*Only "loss" was on penalties in the Nations League Final.*

#### 2026 (5 matches: W2 D3 L0)
| Date | Competition | Match | Score |
|------|------------|-------|-------|
| 27 Mar | Friendly | Spain vs Serbia | 3-0 |
| 31 Mar | Friendly | Spain vs Egypt | 0-0 |
| 4 Jun | Friendly | Spain vs Iraq | 1-1 |
| 8 Jun | Friendly | Peru vs Spain | 1-3 |
| **15 Jun** | **World Cup Group H** | **Spain vs Cape Verde** | **0-0** |

The **0-0 draw with Cape Verde** (World Cup debutants, ranked #67) was described as "one of the shocks of the World Cup" by BBC Sport and ESPN.

### 3. 2026 World Cup Qualification — UEFA Group E
**Status: QUALIFIED as Group E winners** (18 November 2025)

| Pos | Team | Pld | W | D | L | GF | GA | GD | Pts |
|-----|------|-----|---|---|---|----|----|-----|-----|
| **1** | **Spain** | **6** | **5** | **1** | **0** | **21** | **2** | **+19** | **16** |
| 2 | Turkey | 6 | 4 | 1 | 1 | 17 | 10 | +7 | 13 |
| 3 | Georgia | 6 | 2 | 0 | 4 | 8 | 14 | -6 | 6 |
| 4 | Bulgaria | 6 | 1 | 0 | 5 | 4 | 24 | -20 | 3 |

### 4. World Cup Group H Draw
| Team | Pot | FIFA Ranking |
|------|-----|-------------|
| **Spain** | 1 | 2 |
| Uruguay | 2 | 16 |
| Saudi Arabia | 3 | 61 |
| Cape Verde | 4 | 67 |

### 5. Squad & Key Players (26-man squad, announced 25 May 2026)

**Manager: Luis de la Fuente** (appointed Dec 2022; contract through Euro 2028)
- Won **Euro 2024** (7-0 record, beat England 2-1 in final)
- Won **2022-23 Nations League**
- Runner-up 2024-25 Nations League
- Deploys a **4-3-3** emphasizing possession with more crosses than the traditional tiki-taka

**Key Players:**
| Player | Age | Club | Caps | Goals | Role |
|--------|-----|------|------|-------|------|
| **Rodri (C)** | 29 | Man City | 62 | 4 | Midfield anchor; returned from ACL injury |
| **Lamine Yamal** | 18 | Barcelona | 25 | 6 | Right winger; generational talent |
| **Pedri** | 23 | Barcelona | 41 | 6 | Creative midfielder |
| **Mikel Merino** | 29 | Arsenal | 43 | 10 | Box-to-box; top qualifying scorer (7 goals) |
| **Nico Williams** | 23 | Athletic Bilbao | 30 | 6 | Left winger; electric pace |
| **Mikel Oyarzabal** | 29 | Real Sociedad | 53 | 25 | Striker; most prolific active scorer |
| **Dani Olmo** | 28 | Barcelona | 50 | 12 | Attacking midfielder |
| **Pau Cubarsí** | 19 | Barcelona | 12 | 0 | Centre-back; emerging star |

### 6. Form Trajectory & Assessment

**Strengths:**
- Extraordinary squad depth and quality at virtually every position
- Proven tournament-winning pedigree (Euro 2024, Nations League 2023)
- Devastating attacking talent (Yamal, Williams, Pedri, Olmo, Oyarzabal)
- Dominant qualifying campaign (+19 GD, 21 goals scored, 2 conceded)

**Concerns:**
- Pre-World Cup friendly form was inconsistent (2W, 2D in 4 matches)
- 0-0 draw with Cape Verde (WC debutants) signals struggles against deep defensive blocks
- Rodri's sharpness after ACL recovery remains a question mark
- 8 of 26 squad members from Barcelona — potential over-reliance
- Several very young players (Yamal 18, Cubarsí 19, Gavi 21) still gaining major tournament experience
- Dropped from FIFA #1 to #2 in the spring of 2026

**Upcoming Matches:**
- 21 June: Spain vs Saudi Arabia (Atlanta) — must-win
- 26 June: Uruguay vs Spain (Zapopan, Mexico) — likely decisive for group positioning

key_facts: Spain are ranked #2 in the FIFA Men's World Rankings as of 11 June 2026, having dropped from #1 (held September 2025–April 2026) behind Argentina.
Spain qualified for the 2026 World Cup as UEFA Group E winners with a dominant record: W5 D1 L0, 21 goals scored, 2 conceded, +19 goal difference, 16 points.
Spain won Euro 2024 with a perfect 7-0 record under manager Luis de la Fuente, beating England 2-1 in the final.
Spain also won the 2022-23 UEFA Nations League and were runners-up in the 2024-25 Nations League (lost to Portugal on penalties).
Spain's 2026 World Cup opener ended in a 0-0 draw against debutants Cape Verde (ranked #67), widely described as a shock result.
Pre-tournament friendly form was mixed: beat Serbia 3-0 and Peru 3-1, but drew 0-0 with Egypt and 1-1 with Iraq.
Manager Luis de la Fuente deploys a 4-3-3 formation emphasizing possession with more crossing than traditional tiki-taka; his contract runs through Euro 2028.

Key squad members include captain Rodri (Man City, 62 caps, returning from ACL injury), Lamine Yamal (Barcelona, 18 years old, 25 caps), Pedri (Barcelona, 41 caps), Mikel Merino (Arsenal, 43 caps, top qualifying scorer with 7 goals), and Mikel Oyarzabal (Real Sociedad, 53 caps, 25 goals).
Spain are in World Cup Group H with Uruguay (Pot 2), Saudi Arabia (Pot 3), and Cape Verde (Pot 4).
The 26-man squad was announced on 25 May 2026 and includes 8 Barcelona players, raising questions about tactical over-dependence on one club.
Spain were seeded in Pot 1 for the World Cup draw (November 2025) as the #1 ranked team at that time.
